_meta.description: ProphetMap stock universe — manually-defined static fields only. Dynamic pricing scores written to data/scores/YYYY-MM-DD.json by scripts/update-valuations.js

fieldGuide.physicalConstraint: 1=pure software, 5=hard physical monopoly with multi-year lead times
fieldGuide.aiContribution: 0.0-1.0, estimated % of forward revenue/earnings growth attributable to AI thesis
fieldGuide.layerRole: primary=core layer representative, secondary=partial exposure, bridge=spans multiple layers
fieldGuide.supplyChainEvidence: OPTIONAL array of {claim, source, type}. EXTERNAL proof the ticker actually occupies the chokepoint it is mapped to — patent cross-reference, teardown/BOM, regulatory filing, third-party report. type ∈ {patent|teardown|filing|thirdparty|contract}. NEVER company IR self-report or press release. Purpose: separate verified chain position from narrative. Absence = thesis rests on management's word.
fieldGuide.catalysts: OPTIONAL array of {date, event, source}. Concrete dated events backing timeToRealize (e.g. a named platform's mass-production quarter, a capacity-online date, a contract decision window). date in YYYY-MM or YYYY-Qn. Replaces vague near/mid/far with falsifiable timeline. A passed catalyst date with no thesis movement is itself a falsification signal.
fieldGuide.moatCapture: OPTIONAL 1-5. WHO captures the private-correctness moat (orthogonal to physicalConstraint, which covers PHYSICAL lead-time only). 1=pure supplier, the moat accrues to the incumbent above (component vendor) or the customer below; the ticker is a replaceable vendor (the 'sold to whoever has the most compute' trap). 3=contested/shared, capture undecided. 5=the ticker itself holds the private ground truth / license / liability / user habit that a smarter general model still cannot enter. Source: Sarah Guo 2026 moat thesis — 'moat exists' != 'startup captures it'. A physicalConstraint=1 ticker can be moatCapture=5 (e.g. a licensed, liability-bearing, workflow-embedded app). Absence = not yet assessed; funnel falls back to the physical gate alone.
fieldGuide.moatLocks: OPTIONAL array, subset of {licensing, liability, privateData, userHabit, integration}. The NON-physical locks backing moatCapture. Physical lead-time is NOT listed here — it lives in physicalConstraint, do not double-count. Empty/absent with low moatCapture = supplier with no defensible lock.
fieldGuide.moatFalsification: OPTIONAL string. Falsifiable condition under which the moat is shown to accrue elsewhere or be absorbed by the model layer (e.g. 'a general model + public data replicates 80% of the function' or 'the incumbent in-houses it'). Same falsification discipline as thesisFalsification.

funnelPassCriteria.defensibility: physicalConstraint>=4 OR moatCapture>=4 (survives commoditization via a PHYSICAL chokepoint OR a non-physical moat — either suffices)
funnelPassCriteria.supplierTrapWarning: moatCapture<=2 flags a supplier-trap (moat accrues upstream/downstream) — WARNING only, not an auto-fail
